content:
The Backend Revolution Big Tech is Quietly Preparing For

6 min read
Just now

--

For almost a decade, Docker became the default answer to one question. “How do we deploy software?” That answer might not stay true for much longer.
If you’ve worked in backend engineering anytime in the last ten years, chances are your architecture diagram looked something like this.
Application.
Docker container.
Kubernetes.
Cloud.
Repeat a few hundred times.
Containers solved an enormous problem. They gave developers a predictable environment that behaved the same on a laptop, inside CI pipelines, and in production. They made microservices practical and turned Kubernetes into one of the most important technologies in cloud computing.
But here’s something interesting.
The biggest companies building cloud infrastructure aren’t spending all of their time making containers better anymore.
They’re spending a lot of time preparing for what comes after them.
That “something” is WebAssembly.
Most developers still think WebAssembly belongs inside browsers. In reality, it has quietly escaped the…
